raw_intake.concern_narrative: A frontline pharmacist submits a QRE/general question form stating that a customer reports their pet refuses a flavored oral liquid compound. No vomiting, injury, hospitalization, death, contamination, dispensing error, wrong medication, or veterinarian allegation of harm is reported.

review_summary.customer_context_summary: The available case context describes routine palatability or flavor refusal without reported harm or product defect.
missing_information: Whether the pet has previously accepted this flavor
Whether administration technique or food mixing was attempted
Whether the customer wants counseling or alternate dosage form discussion
evidence_limitations: Inventory inspection and trend scan depend on operational data not available to the public RAG corpus.
The synthetic case does not establish whether customer outreach has already occurred.

derived_assessment.rationale: The review describes flavor refusal without vomiting, injury, hospitalization, death, contamination, dispensing error, wrong medication, or veterinarian allegation of harm. After standard record, lot/batch, inventory-if-available, and trend review show no concerning finding, the supported path is guidance or counseling and possible alternate dosage form discussion rather than leadership escalation, refund, reship, or frontline delegation.
